creator: tamelingdaniel@gmail.com
text: If you still have the boot environments from the previous attempts, I would use them to figure out how to fix the issue and leave the main environment untouched. That way what you do can be easily reverted. You can select the enviroment in the boot loader or you use "bectl activate -t ENVIRONMENT". The -t means that it will be active only for the next boot, so if you reboot your healthy system should come back up.
